description_long: This object consists of two parallel concrete jersey barriers, each with a rectangular profile and a slightly tapered top edge. The barriers are primarily gray, with a yellowish trim or coating along the top and side edges, possibly for increased visibility. The surfaces appear rough and unfinished, typical of cast concrete, and there are no visible markings, reflective strips, or warning signs. The barriers are set on flat bases, which help stabilize them and prevent tipping. The ends of the barriers are blunt and squared off, with no openings or interactive features. These barriers are commonly used for traffic control, construction site protection, or to separate lanes on roads and highways.
description: A pair of gray concrete jersey barriers with yellow trim, featuring rough surfaces and flat bases, used for traffic or access control.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad, flat faces of both barriers with yellow trim along the top edges.
description_view_1: Top-down view revealing the tapered sides and the yellow trim running along the length of the barriers.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the flat faces and yellow trim, with both barriers aligned.
description_view_3: Top-down view from the opposite end, again highlighting the tapered sides and yellow trim.
